main.name: The Last Giant
main.description: A large and ancient giant encountered in the Forest of Fallen Giants. Located down a wooden lift behind the locked door at the bottom of the ladder to the Cardinal Tower bonfire. The player will need to navigate the area past this bonfire in order to open the locked door and create a shortcut.

main.name: Dragonrider
main.description: One of the Bosses of Heide's Tower of Flame, the Dragonrider guards the passage way to No-Mans Wharf. Masterless Glencour can be summoned right outside the fog gate to assist with this fight.

main.name: The Pursuer
main.description: After defeating the last giant and taking his key, follow the path in the Forest of Fallen Giants.

main.name: Old Dragonslayer
main.description: Boss behind the fog gate across a large drawbridge dropped by pulling a nearby lever, at Heide's Tower Of Flame. in which a dragon is guarding.

main.name: Flexile Sentry
main.description: Area boss of No-man's Wharf. Accessed by ringing a bell atop the Wharf that docks a ship.

main.name: Lost Sinner
main.description: She is located deep within the lost bastion, in a dark cell. If she is killed in New Game+, her soul will be different when she dies.

main.name: Ruin Sentinels
main.description: Area bosses of Lost Bastille. Accessed by the end of a hallway of a series of cell rooms.

main.name: Belfry Gargoyles
main.description: They are also in an area guarded by the invasion-themed PvP Covenant Bell Keepers, so reaching them in online mode can be challenging.

main.name: Skeleton Lords
main.description: Huntsman's Copse, behind the waterfall area (past caves or narrow passage with red phantom).

main.name: Executioner's Chariot
main.description: Situated in the Huntsman's Copse beyond a decaying wooden bridge and guarded by a hostile dark spirit NPC, The Executioner's Chariot awaits.

main.name: Rato de moicano e sua gangue
main.description: Upon traversing the mist beyond the second bonfire, the Royal Rat Vanguard awaits. Despite its outwardly regular rat appearance, it distinguishes itself with a notable giant mohawk on its back.

main.name: Covetous Demon
main.description: This large monster is encountered in the first floor of Earthen Peak right after crossing Harvest Valley, after defeating him the player will unlock the Lower Earthen Peak bonfire.

main.name: Royal Rat Authority
main.description: It is located at Ordeal's End bonfire, which can be reached through Doors of Pharros.

main.name: Mytha, the Baneful Queen
main.description: Area boss of the Earthen Peak, directly in the middle of Harvest Valley.

main.name: Smelter Demon
main.description: The Smelter Demon awaits as a potential initial boss encounter within the Iron Keep. He is optional so if you have trouble dealing with him, it is better to come back to him after some time. If defeated, returning to his room will result in a Pursuer fight.

main.name: Old Iron King
main.description: The second boss of Iron Keep; there is a nearby bonfire called Eygil's Idol, which is hidden at the top of a ladder in the room with flaming minotaur heads and spikes before the fog gate.

main.name: Scorpioness Najka
main.description: She is found in the Shaded Ruins area of the Shaded Woods. To reach this fight, one must pass by many of the curse jars and lion guards, and for that reason it is a good idea to summon a phantom to assist immediately at the Shaded Ruins bonfire. It takes a branch from the past to reach your area.

main.name: Prowling Magus and Congregation
main.description: Perhaps the weakest boss in the game, located in Brightstone Cove Tseldora.

main.name: Duke's Dear Freja
main.description: Area boss of Brightstone Cove Tseldora. Accessed by going through the whole map. Killing her in New Game+ grants the Moonlight Greatsword.

main.name: The Rotten
main.description: Area boss of the Black Gulch, below The Gutter. the lowest place in the well

main.name: Looking Glass Knight
main.description: Located in the King's Passage, at the top of the castle. Defeating him in New Game+ grants you the Ring of Steel Protection +2.

main.name: Twin Dragonrider
main.description: The game's most difficult boss, now in double dose. Located in Drangleic Castle.

main.name: Demon of Song
main.description: A giant, grotesque frog demon with a corpse skull and arms protruding from the sides of its mouth, and a long tail. Upon entering the boss area you can find the Demon of Song sitting and waiting, as if to lure prey in. The face will use its corpse arms to pull the upper and lower jaw of its body together to hide and protect itself, whilst making itself look like as if it is imitating an actual frog.

main.name: Guardian Dragon
main.description: a red wyvern trapped in a large cage at the top of Aldia's Keep, which must be defeated to proceed to the Dragon Aerie. Different from the previous game, cutting its tail does not reward the player with any item whatsoever, only preventing it from using the Tail Whip attack. This boss is encountered multiple times later as an enemy, usually protecting items and crystal lizards in rock nests.

main.name: Ancient Dragon
main.description: Climb up through the Dragon Shrine past Aldia's Guard. The Ancient Dragon at the top can be avoided, but it drops an important item for the ending.

main.name: DarkLurker
main.description: Secret and optional boss of Dark Chasm of Old (accessed by joining the Pilgrims of Dark Covenant and lighting all 3 fires in the 3 Dark Old Zones, and jumping into the hole that usually is the exit in any of the 3 maps). This boss is only accessible by joining the Pilgrims of Dark covenant through Darkdiver Grandahl and completing its questline.

main.name: Velstadt, the Royal Aegis
main.description: Access the Crypt of the Dead through the Shrine of Amana. Velstadt guards the king's tomb — avoid touching the bells so as not to strengthen the boss.

main.name: Vendrick
main.description: After Velstadt, access the secret chamber. Vendrick patrols without attacking — collect Giant Souls to reduce his defense before the confrontation.

main.name: Giant Lord
main.description: Use the Soul of the Last Giant in the Forest of Giants to access the Memories. The Giant Lord is in the battle memory atop the ruins.

main.name: Throne Watcher & Throne Defender
main.description: Access the Throne of Desire with the four souls of the lords. The guardians must be defeated simultaneously or they will be resurrected.

main.name: Nashandra
main.description: The final boss of the base game. Immediately after the guardians, Nashandra reveals her true form. Keep your distance from the Abyss she projects.

main.name: Aldia, Scholar of the First Sin
main.description: Exclusive boss for Scholar of the First Sin. Appears after Nashandra IF you have spoken to Aldia in three different locations. The true ending of the game.

dlc.name: Elana, the Squalid Queen
dlc.area: Sunken King DLC – Shulva, Sanctum City
dlc.description: Access Shulva through the Sunken King DLC. Elana can summon Velstadt during the fight — eliminate the summoned monster quickly and focus on the quee

dlc.name: Sinh, the Slumbering Dragon
dlc.description: The final boss of the Sunken King DLC. The dragon sleeps on the altar — upon awakening, it uses a lethal combination of toxic gas and physical attacks.

dlc.name: Afflicted Graverobber, Varg & Cerah
dlc.area: Sunken King DLC – Cave of the Dead
dlc.description: A trio of humans in the Sunken King DLC. Each has a different fighting style — isolate them one by one and use the environment to your advantage.

dlc.name: Aava, the King's Pet
dlc.area: Old Ivory King DLC – Frozen Eleum Loyce
dlc.description: The guardian from the Ivory King DLC. Aava is invisible without the correct item — find the Eye of the Priestess in the area to reveal the boss.

dlc.name: Burnt Ivory King
dlc.area: Old Ivory King DLC – Chaos Tomb
dlc.description: The final boss of the DLC. Defeat lost knights in the area to summon allies to the fight. The King emerges from the chaos with his armor ablaze.

dlc.name: Lud and Zallen, the King's Pets
dlc.area: Old Ivory King DLC – Frigid Outskirts
dlc.description: Two big cats in a dense blizzard. Considered one of the most difficult bosses in the game due to the weather conditions and the double fight.

dlc.name: Fume Knight
dlc.area: Old Iron King DLC – Brume Tower
dlc.description: Raime, the Knight of Flames. Considered the most difficult boss in the game. his second phase covers the arena in flames. Patience is essential.
dlc.dlc: Crown of the Old Iron King

dlc.name: Sir Alonne
dlc.area: Old Iron King DLC – Memory of the Old Iron King
dlc.description: The legendary knight of Raime. Accessible through the Memory of the Iron King. Extremely elegant and technical fighting style
dlc.dlc: Crown of the Old Iron King

dlc.name: Smelter Demon (Blue)
dlc.area: Old Iron King DLC – Brume Tower
dlc.description: A bluer, more aggressive version of the original Smelter Demon. Found in a corridor of the Brume Tower — same pattern, but with more damage.
dlc.dlc: Crown of the Old Iron King
